steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Take the positive square root (all variables are positive): $x = \frac{\sqrt{5fg}}{12}$. Now evaluate each choice.
  • $x = \frac{\sqrt{5fg}}{12}$ matches $x = \frac{\sqrt{5fg}}{12}$ exactly. That matches Choice A — $x=\frac{\sqrt{5fg}}{12}$.
  • $x = 12\sqrt{5fg}$ would give $x^{2} = 720fg$, but we need $x^{2} = \frac{5fg}{144}$. The 12 belongs in the denominator, not multiplied on top. Not correct.
  • $x = \sqrt{\frac{5fg}{12}}$ would give $x^{2} = \frac{5fg}{12}$, but the denominator should be 144 (since $(12x)^{2}$ contributes a factor of $12^{2}$). Not correct.
  • $x = \sqrt{5fg} - 12$ squaring gives $5fg - 24\sqrt{5fg} + 144$, which cannot equal $\frac{5fg}{144}$ in general. Not correct.
